title: Write Miss Handling Algorithm
description: Pseudocode demonstrating the decision logic for handling a write miss with allocation policy selection
code:
PROCEDURE HandleWriteMiss(address, data)
 blockOffset = address % BLOCK_SIZE
 setIndex = (address / BLOCK_SIZE) % NUM_SETS
 
 // Check if block is in any line of the set
 FOR each way IN cacheSet[setIndex]
 IF way.tag == address.tag AND way.valid == TRUE
 // Write hit - update existing line
 way.data[blockOffset] = data
 IF writePolicy == WRITE_BACK
 way.dirty = TRUE
 RETURN
 END FOR
 
 // Write miss - decide allocation policy
 IF allocationPolicy == WRITE_ALLOCATE
 // Fetch block from memory
 block = ReadFromMemory(address)
 
 // Find victim line to replace if cache is full
 victim = SelectEvictionVictim(cacheSet[setIndex])
 
 // Write back if dirty
 IF victim.dirty == TRUE
 WriteToMemory(victim.tag, victim.data)
 
 // Allocate new block
 victim.tag = address.tag
 victim.data = block
 victim.valid = TRUE
 victim.dirty = FALSE
 
 // Perform the write
 victim.data[blockOffset] = data
 IF writePolicy == WRITE_BACK
 victim.dirty = TRUE
 ELSE
 // No-write allocate: write directly to memory
 WriteToMemory(address, data)
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This algorithm shows how write misses are handled based on the allocation policy. For write allocate, it fetches the block, possibly evicts a victim, and writes to cache. For no-write allocate, it bypasses the cache and writes directly to main memory.

complexity.time: O(1) average, O(n) for eviction check

title: Cache Line Fill Operation
description: Pseudocode showing the cache line fill process when allocating on a miss
code:
PROCEDURE CacheLineFill(setIndex, tag, address)
 // Select an available line in the set
 targetLine = FindEmptyLine(cacheSet[setIndex])
 
 IF targetLine == NULL
 // Cache is full - need eviction
 targetLine = EvictionPolicy.SelectVictim(cacheSet[setIndex])
 
 // Write back if dirty
 IF targetLine.dirty == TRUE
 memAddress = ConstructAddress(targetLine.tag, setIndex)
 MemoryController.Write(memAddress, targetLine.data)
 targetLine.dirty = FALSE
 END IF
 
 // Fetch block from memory
 blockStartAddress = (address / BLOCK_SIZE) * BLOCK_SIZE
 targetLine.data = MemoryController.ReadBlock(blockStartAddress, BLOCK_SIZE)
 
 // Update line metadata
 targetLine.tag = tag
 targetLine.valid = TRUE
 targetLine.dirty = FALSE
 
 RETURN targetLine
END PROCEDURE
explanation: Cache line fill handles fetching a block from main memory into the cache. It handles both empty cache cases and full cache cases requiring eviction with potential write-back of dirty data.

title: Write Policy and Allocation Policy Combinations
description: Pseudocode demonstrating all four combinations of write and allocation policies
code:
// Combination 1: Write-Through + No-Write Allocate
PROCEDURE WT_NWA_Write(address, data)
 WriteToMemory(address, data) // Bypass cache completely
 InvalidateCacheLine(address) // Ensure consistency
END PROCEDURE

// Combination 2: Write-Through + Write Allocate
PROCEDURE WT_WA_Write(address, data)
 IF CacheHit(address)
 WriteToCache(address, data)
 ELSE
 block = ReadFromMemory(address)
 line = AllocateCacheLine(address, block)
 WriteToCacheLine(line, address, data)
 END IF
 WriteToMemory(address, data) // Also write through to memory
END PROCEDURE

// Combination 3: Write-Back + No-Write Allocate (rare)
PROCEDURE WB_NWA_Write(address, data)
 WriteToMemory(address, data) // Write directly to memory
 // Cache not involved
END PROCEDURE

// Combination 4: Write-Back + Write Allocate (Most Common)
PROCEDURE WB_WA_Write(address, data)
 IF CacheHit(address)
 WriteToCache(address, data)
 SetDirty(address)
 ELSE
 block = ReadFromMemory(address)
 line = AllocateCacheLine(address, block)
 WriteToCacheLine(line, address, data)
 SetDirty(address)
 END IF
 // Memory write deferred until eviction
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This code demonstrates all four combinations of write policy (write-through/write-back) and allocation policy (write-allocate/no-write-allocate), with the most common being write-back with write-allocate.
